title: andryala dentata subsp. arenaria
intro: Andryala dentata subsp. arenaria, also known as the Mediterranean andryala, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its toothed leaves and yellow flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, and has been traditionally used to treat conditions like fever and rheumatism. Herbal preparations of Andryala dentata subsp. arenaria include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made from the leaves and flowers of the plant.
